description:

Here, the air is scented with sea salt and mountain fynbos, offering a tranquil lifestyle defined by the natural wonders of the Overberg. Whether you envision a modern glass-fronted permanent residence or a cozy weekend hideaway, this stand invites you to trade the city rush for the rhythmic pace of the ocean. Secure your piece of the Kogelberg Biosphere and start building the future you've always imagined.
About Betty's Bay
Betty's Bay is a hidden gem on the scenic Clarence Drive, stretching along a narrow strip between the dramatic Kogelberg Mountains and the Atlantic Ocean. Known for its rugged beauty, it is a town that prioritizes the environment, making it a sanctuary for both people and wildlife.
- UNESCO Heritage: Situated within the Kogelberg Biosphere Reserve, the area is globally recognized for its incredible floral diversity and conservation efforts.
- Outdoor Adventure: From the world-renowned hiking trails of the Harold Porter Botanical Garden to the premier surfing breaks and fishing spots at Silversands and Main Beach, the outdoor possibilities are endless.
- Unique Neighbors: It is home to the Stony Point Penguin Colony, one of the few places in the world where you can observe African Penguins in their natural habitat.
- A Dark-Sky Community: By choice, the town has no streetlights, preserving the "village feel" and ensuring some of the best stargazing in the Western Cape.

Nestled between the mountains and the ocean, Betty's Bay is a quiet, seaside village located within the Kogelberg Biosphere Reserve—a UNESCO World Heritage site. Unlike the busier neighboring towns, Betty's Bay has maintained a rugged, natural charm with no streetlights and wide-open spaces.
- Nature andamp; Wildlife: Home to the famous Stony Point African Penguin colony and the breathtaking Harold Porter Botanical Gardens
- Outdoor Lifestyle: A haven for hikers, surfers, and nature lovers, offering everything from sandboarding on the dunes to peaceful walks around four freshwater lakes
- Location: Situated along the scenic R44 (Clarence Drive), it is a comfortable 60-minute drive from Cape Town International Airport, making it an ideal weekend retreat or a permanent sanctuary.

descriptionHeader: Family home in a peaceful neighbourhood with all that you need.

The family bathroom has a bath and a guest toilet, with the main bedroom having an ensuite bathroom with shower.  The private back garden has large established trees as well as a water feature offers a tranquil retreat for relaxing afternoons in the shade.

Grabouw is famous for it's pear and apple producing orchards and now also for cool climate wines and nature lovers weekend getaways.  Our farm stalls are popular as are the quaint wineries and eateries in the area and don't forget about the Elgin Railway Market where the Ceres steam train stops off on most weekends.
